strategy_intro.headline: We were unable to access detailed information about this Member's appropriations request form.
strategy_intro.body:
This below information is based on the typical structure and questions found in appropriations request forms. It should provide enough context to submit your request. Please use your best judgment to adapt the information in this guide to fit the unique requirements of your Member's form. If you have any questions, contact Jack Kiraly at jack.kiraly@planetary.org.

Key Points:
key_points: Ask: $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (24% above FY 2026 enacted)
Coalition: Coordinated with AAS, AGU, and other science advocacy partners
Process: Your submission informs what your Member formally enters into the congressional record
Impact: Over 100 submissions last year helped Congress reject proposed cuts

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Use the guide below to complete your representative's FY 2027 appropriations request form. The guide is organized to match the typical structure of these forms. Most content is pre-filled based on The Planetary Society's official FY 2027 position — your most important contribution is personalizing the state/district impact section with your unique local connection to NASA Science.

sections.description: Forms require your personal contact details to verify you are a constituent of this Member's state or district. Offices will only consider requests made by their constituents.

fields.label: Your Personal Information
fields.draft_value: Forms will typically ask for your first and last name, your home address, phone number, and and email.

fields.label: If Asked for Organizational Information
fields.draft_value:
Sometimes, the form requests an organizational affiliation or a separate organizational point of contact. If required, you may use the following:

Organization: The Planetary Society
Address: 60 S. Los Robles Ave, Pasadena, CA 91101
Website: planetary.org
Organizational contact: Jack Kiraly, Director of Government Relations, jack.kiraly@planetary.org

Some forms ask for a "secondary point of contact", if so, Jack Kiraly is the appropriate contact to list there.

sections.description: These fields tell Congress exactly which budget line-item you are requesting. They ensure your submission is routed to the correct appropriations subcommittee and formally attributed to the right account.

fields.label: Which appropriations bill is this request for?
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science and Related Agencies (CJS)
fields.rationale: NASA is funded through the CJS Appropriations Bill.

fields.label: What appropriations subcommittee funds this program?
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science and Related Agencies Subcommittee
fields.rationale: NASA is funded through the CJS Subcommittee.

fields.label: What federal agency administers this program?
fields.draft_value: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)
fields.rationale: Drawn directly from the asks configuration.

fields.label: Within this agency, what is the specific program or account you are making a request for?
fields.draft_value: Science
fields.rationale: Exact account name from the reference values.

sections.description: This is our formal ask for funding in FY 2027. All values here reflect The Planetary Society's official FY 2027 position and should be used as-is.
sections.coach_note: This is a programmatic funding request, not a congressionally directed spending (earmark) request. If a form asks about what type of request this is, make sure to select 'Programmatic', 'Funding request', or similar.

fields.label: A short title for your request
fields.draft_value: Funding for NASA's Science Mission Directorate
fields.rationale: Exact short title from the reference values.

fields.label: A short description of your request
fields.draft_value: This request would fund NASA's Science Mission Directorate at $9 billion.
fields.rationale: Drawn directly from the asks configuration.

fields.label: What type of request are you making?
fields.help_text: Select 'Funding request' or 'Programmatic' — NOT 'Congressionally Directed Spending' or 'Earmark.' This is a request to fund an existing agency account, not to direct funds to a specific project.
fields.draft_value: 'Funding request', 'Programmatic', or similar
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for the NASA SMD topline.

fields.label: Funding amount requested
fields.rationale: Exact requested funding amount for FY27 from the asks configuration.

fields.label: Report language requested
fields.draft_value: N/A
fields.rationale: No specific report language is requested.

fields.label: Bill language requested
fields.draft_value: N/A
fields.rationale: No specific bill language is requested.

fields.label: Is this a new or existing program?
fields.draft_value: Existing program
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is a long-standing existing program.

sections.description: This is the heart of your submission. It's an opportunity for you to make the case about the local benefits of NASA Science, either through contract and award information (provided above) or even from the inspiration and broad benefits provided by the activity.
sections.coach_note: We provide a starting point based on local economic impacts, but don't just copy and paste these. Rewrite them in your own voice and add your own perspective.

fields.label: Please describe the problem or issue your request will address / goals, results, and/or expected outcome
fields.help_text: Forms may use one of several variations of this question — adapt the pre-filled text as needed. If there is no separate space for state/district impact, add a few sentences about that impact here before submitting.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science is on the verge of launching major missions — Roman Space Telescope, Dragonfly, NEO Surveyor, DAVINCI, VERITAS — that represent decades of investment. The FY 2026 budget chaos caused real damage: 4,000+ civil servants pushed out, contractors lost, a 25% drop in new grants, and months wasted on termination planning instead of mission execution. Congress rejected those cuts for good reason. Another round of steep cuts in FY 2027 would repeat the same wasteful cycle and undermine priorities Congress already settled.

* Other nations are investing heavily in their own space science programs. The question isn't whether space science will advance, it's whether America will be the one leading it. $9 billion for NASA Science helps restore the workforce, partnerships, and breakthrough missions that makes American leadership possible.

* NASA Science is an innovation engine for the nation, and benefits all 50 states. These investments turbocharge the American economy and have real benefits from Earth observation, disaster response, national security, and fundamental scientific breakthroughs.
fields.rationale: Drawn directly from the provided justification text. Add a few sentences about why this matters to your state/district before submitting.

fields.label: Identify which part(s) of your request may be of particular benefit to this Member's state/district
fields.help_text: Describe specific NASA investments in your area: research grants to local universities, contracts with businesses in your district, STEM education programs, or your own work or inspiration enabled by NASA Science. Be concrete — dollar amounts and institution names are more persuasive than general statements.
fields.draft_value:
Use the provided information about NASA's scientific grants and awards to highlight some of the local benefits to your region.

You can also browse more information at the NASA Science Economic Impact Dashboard:

 dashboards.planetary.org/nasa-science

sections.description: Prior-year funding levels provide appropriators with essential baseline context. These numbers tell the story of a program that has faced significant budget pressure and demonstrate that the congressional record already supports protecting it.
sections.coach_note: These numbers give critical context and establish a pattern of consistent bipartisan support for NASA Science. Lean into that.

fields.label: If requesting funding, what was this program funded at in the FY 2026 bill?
fields.rationale: FY 2026 enacted level from the reference values.

fields.label: FY 2023 enacted funding level
fields.rationale: FY 2023 enacted level from the reference values.

fields.label: FY 2024 enacted funding level
fields.rationale: FY 2024 enacted level from the reference values.

fields.label: FY 2025 President's Budget Request
fields.rationale: FY 2025 President's Budget Request from the reference values.

fields.label: FY 2025 enacted funding level
fields.rationale: FY 2025 enacted level from the reference values.

fields.label: FY 2026 President's Budget Request
fields.rationale: FY 2026 President's Budget Request from the reference values.

fields.label: Net increase or decrease over FY 2027 President's Budget Request
fields.draft_value: Currently unknown (President's Budget Request has not been released)
fields.rationale: The FY 2027 PBR has not yet been released.

fields.label: Net increase or decrease over FY 2026 enacted funding level
fields.rationale: The $9B request represents a $1.75B or 24% increase over the FY 2026 enacted $7.25B.

sections.description: Sometimes a form will ask if there are other elected officials supporting your request. This helps appropriations offices understand the political landscape and helps to establish credibility.
sections.coach_note: Make sure to mention the internal letter circulating in the House led by Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) that is asking for $9 billion for NASA Science. It serves as an important bipartisan signal demonstrates that there is   broad support for your request.

fields.label: Are you placing this programmatic appropriations request with any other members of Congress? If yes, who?
fields.help_text: List any other House or Senate offices you are submitting or planning to submit to. For example: 'Sen. [Name] (Senate) and Rep. [Name] (House, District).' Submitting to both your House representative and both your Senators doubles or triples the impact of your advocacy.
fields.draft_value: No

fields.label: Are you requesting that this Member lead a multi-Member letter in support?
fields.draft_value: No.
fields.rationale: We are not asking members to lead a new letter.

fields.label: Is there an existing multi-Member letter to sign onto for this request? If so, what is the last name of the Member leading the letter?
fields.draft_value: Yes, for House: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA). Not yet for Senate.
fields.rationale: An existing House Dear Colleague letter is currently circulating.

fields.label: What other organizations might be in favor of this request?
fields.draft_value:
American Geophysical Union
American Society for Gravitational and Space Research
Black in AstroCoalition for Aerospace and Science
Planetary Science Institute
Satellite Industry Association
The Mars Society
The Planetary Society
fields.rationale: Organizations listed in the reference values as supporting this request.

fields.label: What other organizations might be opposed to this request?
fields.draft_value: None
fields.rationale: No organizations are expected to oppose this request.
